ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: And the man said: Who are you? And he said: I am Betuel, the son of Nahor and Milka, and I am the brother of your mother;

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: And it came to pass after these things that Joseph's brother died, and all his family and all his brothers came to mourn him, and Joseph wept for his brother.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: And Jacob said to them: This is what the children of Israel did to me: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and you want to take Benjamin away from me also; everything is against me!

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: It is Adam who says: If a person has died and is buried in the ground, he will be raised from the ground on the day of resurrection; the one who has done good will see it, and the one who has done evil will see it.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.

translated: And the man asked: Where are you going? And he said: I am going to Betuel, to Nahor's family, to find a wife for my master's son; and I and the camels are tired, and there is no place for us to spend the night;

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,

translated: And it came to pass when Joseph's brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peaceably to him. And Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it to his brothers.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”

translated: And Jacob said to them: Bring me your youngest brother that I may know that you are not spies; I have Joseph, I have Simeon, and I have taken your brother Benjamin, bring your youngest brother to me

translated: Adam said: If a person has died and is buried in the ground, that person will be raised; for just as the grain of corn dies, it is raised and bears fruit.

translated: And it came to pass after these things that Joseph's master took him and put him in the prison, a place where the king's prisoners were kept. And Joseph was there in the prison.

translated: It is Adam who says: If you have a sheep, give me a sheep, and if you have a goat, give me a goat; if you have yams, give me yams, and if you have corn, give me corn.

translated: And it came to pass after these things that Joseph's master took notice of him, and saw that the Lord was with him, and that the Lord made all that he did to prosper in his hand, and Joseph found favour in his sight.

translated: And Jacob said to them: Bring me some wild game so I may prepare it: Joseph is coming, and Simeon is coming, and the youngest Benjamin is coming; let us eat and be merry!
